title: Offline edits accumulate locally and replay to Swarm on reconnect via the outbox cursor.
content:
## Claim
Offline-edit durability is provided by PGlite + the outbox cursor: the local reactor keeps working, and on reconnect the cursor replays accumulated ops to Swarm.

## Flow
1. User edits documents while Bee node is unreachable.
2. Operations accumulate in PGlite (local reactor works fine).
3. SwarmChannel connection state transitions to `error` → push is blocked.
4. User reconnects Bee node → state becomes `connected`.
5. Outbox cursor catches up: all accumulated ops are pushed to Swarm.
6. No data loss — cursors in PGlite track exactly what was synced.

## Why this is safe
- The cursor is only advanced after a successful feed write, so a crash mid-push is idempotent.
- The local reactor is the source of truth; Swarm is purely a mirror.
- User never loses work due to Bee downtime.

## Source
Swarm Channel Architecture, "Flow 4: Offline Edits → Reconnect".
description: When Bee is unreachable the channel goes to error; ops pile up in PGlite; on reconnect the outbox cursor catches up, replaying everything with no data loss.
